title: gladiolus aurantiacus
intro: Gladiolus aurantiacus, also known as the orange gladiolus, is a species of flowering plant in the family Iridaceae. It is primarily native to the mountainous regions of South Africa and Lesotho, where it grows in rocky outcrops and grasslands. Gladiolus aurantiacus has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory problems, and can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions to harness its health benefits.
